channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: South Africa’s crisis of powerlessness, corruption and misplaced anger towards migrants
item.description: This opinion piece reflects on how power and powerlessness shape social behaviour in South Africa, particularly in the context of rising anti-migrant sentiment. The author uses a simple but powerful a...

item.title: Sting suggests decline in manual labour may be influencing modern masculinity and social behaviour
item.description: Sting has suggested that the decline of manual, physically demanding jobs could be contributing to traits often associated with so-called toxic masculinity. In an interview, the musician reflected on ...

item.title: Audit reveals Defence recovered fraud-related debt from employee and identified weaknesses in misconduct investigations
item.description: An audit of the Department of Defence's investigation and misconduct management processes has revealed that a public servant was required to repay approximately $14,800 after being found to have commi...

item.title: FIA penalises officers over misuse of authority in passenger offloading cases
item.description: According to a report presented to the Senate, the Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) has taken disciplinary action against multiple immigration officers for misuse of authority in cases involving the...

item.title: Pakistani freelancers earn over $950m in foreign exchange in 10 months, outperform regional peers
item.description: According to data released by the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP), Pakistani freelancers have generated more than $950 million in foreign exchange during the first 10 months of the fiscal year 2025-26 (1...

item.title: KwaZulu-Natal police ready to maintain order amid truck and anti-foreigner protests
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal police have stated they are fully prepared to manage planned protests by truck drivers and anti-foreigner marches over the upcoming weekend. Acting Provincial Commissioner Major General ...

item.title: ABS-CBN Feature Reflects on 2NE1's Lasting Influence on K-pop
item.description: The article highlights the enduring impact of 2NE1, one of the most influential girl groups in K-pop history. Based on the available information, the feature looks back at how the group helped reshape...

item.title: Opposition Parties Criticise Tinubu Administration Over Rising Hardship and Insecurity
item.description: Di African Democratic Congress (ADC) and di Social Democratic Party (SDP) presidential candidate, Adewole Adebayo, don yan say President Bola Tinubu administration dey make life hard for Nigerians. De...

item.title: Government to widen audit of CBSE on-screen marking system following concerns over implementation issues
item.description: The Union education ministry is preparing to expand its scrutiny of the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) on-screen marking (OSM) system, moving towards a comprehensive audit of the platform...

item.title: Blue Jays rally from five-run deficit to defeat Orioles 6–5
item.description: The Toronto Blue Jays produced a dramatic late comeback to defeat the Baltimore Orioles 6–5 on Friday night after trailing by five runs entering the seventh inning. Toronto had been held to just two h...

item.title: Tony Yengeni calls for left-wing unity and criticises DA and FF Plus at SACP conference
item.description: MK Party deputy president Tony Yengeni addressed the South African Communist Party’s Conference of the Left in Boksburg, where he urged stronger unity among left-wing and progressive political formati...

item.title: Experts Highlight Link Between Excess Sugar Consumption and Erectile Dysfunction Risk
item.description: Recent health discussions have focused on the possible relationship between excessive sugar consumption and erectile dysfunction in men. According to medical professionals and nutrition experts, high ...

item.title: Hurricanes eliminate Canadiens to advance to Stanley Cup Final after 6–1 Game 5 win
item.description: The Montreal Canadiens’ pursuit of a 25th Stanley Cup came to an end after a decisive 6–1 defeat to the Carolina Hurricanes in Game 5 of the Eastern Conference Final. With the victory, Carolina won th...

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Abolishing Healthwatch and Its Impact on NHS Accountability
item.description: Local councils in England and Wales have expressed serious concerns over the government's plans to abolish Healthwatch, the independent body representing patients' views on health and social care serv...

item.title: Government considers removing 1pc export advance tax in upcoming budget amid industry pressure
item.description: The government is reportedly considering abolishing the 1pc advance tax imposed on exporters in the upcoming federal budget, a move that could provide around Rs100 billion in relief to the export sect...

item.title: Tinubu Defends Fuel Subsidy Removal, Says Economic Reforms Are Improving Nigeria’s Finances
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has defended his administration’s decision to remove fuel subsidy, saying the policy helped Nigeria avoid a serious fiscal crisis and created the foundation for economic recovery...

item.title: San Francisco homeowner sues robotics start-up over property damage during Airbnb stay
item.description: A San Francisco homeowner has filed a lawsuit seeking over $12,000 in damages after a multi-billion-dollar robotics start-up allegedly caused extensive damage to his Airbnb rental. The case, lodged in...

item.title: Cosplay couple Awie and Prince De Guzman share how makeup enhances their relationship
item.description: Cosplay couple Awie and Prince De Guzman recently opened up about how their shared love for makeup has strengthened their relationship. Both passionate about cosplay, the pair spend significant time p...

item.title: 33 Suspects Arraigned Over Ebonyi Communal Clash and Killings
item.description: In Ebonyi State, Nigeria, a state high court in Abakaliki arraigned 33 suspects connected to a deadly communal clash between Amasiri Community in Afikpo Local Government Area and Okporojo community in...

item.title: Supreme Court mandates strict timelines for bail decisions and judgment delivery in High Courts
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has issued strong nationwide directions aimed at reducing judicial delays, particularly in bail matters that involve personal liberty. In a landmark judgment delivered under...
